name: qrc-demo-range-max__2026-05-09
description: Scenario ① "Fires, never resolves" — alert-replay live test against real Grafana Cloud on 2026-05-09. Counter pushed at 8 req/s for ~258s, then stopped. Real Grafana transitioned Normal → Pending @ T0+27s → Firing @ T0+151s → Normal @ T0+650s (the last transition delayed by Grafana Cloud keep_firing_for hysteresis).

provenance.samplesCapturedVia: Grafana Cloud Prometheus query_range API, fetched 2026-05-12 against pantrypulse.grafana.net (the same instance the May 9 run used). Counter retention preserves the run; this is a replay of the exact bytes Grafana's evaluator saw.
provenance.expectedCapturedVia: Hand-transcribed from alert-replay README's "Observed timeline (clean re-test, T0 = 21:30:07)" table — live observations of the Grafana UI during the May 9 run. Grafana state-history API was queried but returned empty (Loki state history not enabled for this rule), so README is the only persistent record of state transitions.
knownDivergencesNotAsserted: Grafana keep_firing_for hysteresis (~272s between rate-below-threshold and Normal). Step 14d.7 added Recovering + keepFiringFor support, but the qrc-demo-range-max rule does NOT set keep_firing_for in its JSON — the ~272s observed on 2026-05-09 came from a Grafana Cloud-level default whose exact value is unknown. This fixture uses keepFiringFor: 0 (the rule's actual configured value) and the test does not assert Resolved timing.

Tick wallclock alignment. Grafana evaluates at its scheduler cadence (independent of when samples started); alert-whatif anchors ticks at samples[0].t. The test allows ±60s tolerance on Pending/Firing entry times — Grafana's own ±60s natural jitter per trigger.interval=1m.
